options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Determines the goal of the seed

Reach Peak: Reach the peak on the specified ascent level

Complete All Badges: Collect the specified number of badges

24 Karat Badge: Toss the Ancient Idol into The Kiln's lava.

Peak and Badges: Reach the peak on the specified ascent level and collect the specified number of badges

options.name: ascent_count
options.description:
The ascent level required to complete the Reach Peak goal (0-7)

Higher ascents add more difficulty modifiers and challenges

options.name: badge_count
options.description:
The number of badges required to complete the Complete All Badges goal

There are 54 total badges available in the game

options.name: progressive_stamina
options.description:
Enable progressive stamina bars

Players start with 25% stamina and unlock 25% more with each upgrade until reaching 100%

options.name: additional_stamina_bars
options.description:
Enable 4 additional stamina bars beyond the base 100%

Allows players to reach up to 200% total stamina (requires Progressive Stamina to be enabled)

options.name: item_sanity
options.description:
Controls whether items need to be unlocked before they can appear in loot pools.

When enabled, each item type has a corresponding "Unlock" item in the Archipelago pool.
You must receive that unlock before the item can spawn in-game.
"Acquire" checks also require having the unlock item.

When disabled, all items are available from the start.
"Acquire" checks only require biome access.

This option works independently from Loot Sanity - Item Sanity controls the unlock gating,
while Loot Sanity controls which pools the items get shuffled into.

options.name: loot_sanity
options.description:
Controls which loot pools have their contents shuffled with all available items.

Affected pools will have every AP-tracked item added at equal weight,
meaning any item can drop from any affected source.
Items not yet unlocked (when Item Sanity is on) are excluded.

None: Loot pools are unchanged. Items only appear in their vanilla pools.

Luggage: Luggage (suitcases) across all biomes have their contents shuffled.

Trees and Bushes: Natural sources (berry bushes, mushroom clusters, coconut trees,
willow trees, vines, winterberry trees, nests, cacti, redwoods) have their drops shuffled.

All: Both luggage and natural source pools are shuffled.

The Scout Statue is never affected by this option - see Logical Scout Statue instead.

options.name: logical_scout_statue
options.description:
When enabled, the Scout Statue will only drop items that
you haven't sent an "Acquire" check for yet.

The available items are filtered by your current biome access (Progressive Mountain count)
and item unlock status (if Item Sanity is enabled).

As you pick up new items and send Acquire checks, the Scout Statue's pool shrinks,
guiding you toward items you still need.

When disabled, the Scout Statue uses the default game loot table.

options.name: disable_multiplayer_badges
options.description:
Exclude multiplayer-only badge checks. These badges are normally earned by cooperating with other players.
These badges include:
    Ultimate Badge
    Clutch Badge
    Participation Badge
    Emergency Preparedness Badge
    First Aid Badge
    Resourcefulness Badge
    Disaster Response Badge
    Applied Esoterica Badge
    Needlepoint Badge
    Happy Camper Badge

options.name: disable_hard_badges
options.description:
Exclude somewhat hard badge checks. These badges include:
    Speed Climber Badge
    Lone Wolf Badge
    Balloon Badge
    Bing Bong Badge
    Competitive Eating Badge

options.name: disable_biome_badges
options.description:
Exclude biome exclusive badge checks. These will not remove the badges involved with completing the biomes.
These badges include:
    Astronomy Badge
    Bundled Up Badge
    Undead Encounter Badge
    Mycoacrobatics Badge
    Web Security Badge
    Cool Cucumber Badge
    Daredevil Badge
    Megaentomology Badge
    Tread Lightly Badge
    Advanced Mycology Badge

options.name: ring_link
options.description:
When enabled, ring pickups are shared among all players with Ring Link enabled

For PEAK; rings are stamina. Consuming food will send Rings to other players with Ring Link enabled. Poisonous food will send negative rings.

options.name: hard_ring_link
options.description:
When enabled, ring pickups are shared among all players with Hard Ring Link enabled

Similar to Ring Link, but instead of sending rings when consuming food, rings are sent from certain actions and events.

options.name: energy_link
options.description:
When enabled, allows sending and receiving energy from a shared server pool

Players can contribute stamina to help others in need

options.name: trap_link
options.description:
When enabled, traps you receive are also sent to other players with Trap Link enabled

You will also receive any linked traps from other players with Trap Link enabled,
if you have a weight above "none" set for that trap

options.name: breath_link
options.description: When enabled, if any player in the lobby runs out of stamina, all players with Breath Link enabled get their stamina fully depleted.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: death_link_behavior
options.description:
Determines what happens when a Death Link is received

Kill Random Player: A random player in your lobby will be killed

Reset to Last Checkpoint: All players will be teleported to the last checkpoint/campfire

options.name: death_link_send_behavior
options.description:
Determines when Death Links are sent to other players

Any Player Dies: Send Death Link whenever any player in your game dies

All Players Dead: Send Death Link only when all players are dead (game over)

options.name: trap_percentage
options.description: Replace a percentage of junk items in the item pool with random traps

options.name: zoom_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of receiving a trap which drastically changes a player's camera zoom level

options.name: eruption_trap_weight

options.name: beetle_horde_trap_weight

options.name: custom_trivia_trap_weight

options.name: pokemon_count_trap_weight

options.name: inverted_mouse_trap_weight

options.name: stamina_drain_trap_weight

options.name: chaos_control_trap_weight

options.name: emergency_rescue_trap_weight

options.name: explosion_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of receiving a trap which spawns an explosion on a random player

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
